question: what is the vicharanashala internship?
answer: a two-month, full-time engagement at the vicharanashala lab, a research lab at iit ropar. you will work on a real open-source project under a mentor, after a short training phase tailored to where you already are. the internship is free — we do not charge, and the work is real.

question: what is vins?
answer: vins is the vicharanashala internship — an online programme open to anyone who clears our interview. the work is real open-source contribution under a mentor, the certificate is from the vicharanashala lab for education design at iit ropar, and the programme itself is free (we charge nothing). there is no stipend. if you are seeing a yellow vins panel on your result page, you are selected.

question: what are the phases of vins, and what do the badges mean?
answer: vins is structured as four phases.

question: who is the internship for? are alumni eligible?
answer: the internship is for currently-enrolled students at any college or university.

question: is this the same as iit ropar's official summer research internship?
answer: no. summership 2026 is a vled lab initiative.

question: i have to attend my class tomorrow/today/some day — can i take leave?
answer: leave is not permitted.

question: when can i start?
answer: you can start any time in 2026.

question: how long is the internship?
answer: two months from your chosen start date, with an optional one-month grace period.

question: can i start in july, august or later if i have exams now?
answer: yes — but only if your exams genuinely make an earlier start impossible.

question: can i start with the cohort and take a relaxation during my exam window?
answer: no. this is not an arrangement we offer.

question: can i take leave or get an exemption during the internship for an exam scheduled in june?
answer: the attendance rule is firm.

question: are orientation session recordings shared with interns?
answer: recordings of the sessions will not be provided.

question: what dates do i put on the noc?
answer: default: your chosen start date to your start plus 2 months.

question: who can sign the noc?
answer: any authorised signatory at your college: hod, acting hod, principal, dean, director, or t&p officer.

question: when do i submit the noc? is the deadline hard?
answer: there is no specific calendar cut-off date.

question: what format should i use? do i need to design it myself?
answer: no — we provide a printable noc format.

question: what if my college gives me an noc in their own format?
answer: a college's own noc format is acceptable, as long as all required entries are present.

question: does it need to be signed by hand?
answer: yes. three things are required.

question: can my hod email the noc instead of uploading it?
answer: no. your noc must be uploaded by you, the student, from your dashboard.

question: how do i download and upload the noc?
answer: both happen on your dashboard at samagama.in.

question: what if my noc is not formally verified?
answer: noc verification takes time — typically anywhere between an hour and one full working day.

question: my online course (masai, nptel, coursera, etc.) won't issue an noc. what do i do?
answer: the internship is open only to candidates currently enrolled in a full-time degree programme.

question: my hod/college official wants written confirmation before signing my noc. what do i show them?
answer: your selection is already confirmed the moment your yellow vins result panel appears.

question: can prof. sudarshan iyengar or a faculty member from iit ropar sign my noc?
answer: your noc must be signed by an authorised signatory at the institution where you are enrolled.

question: how do i know i am selected?
answer: if you can see your yellow vins result panel on samagama.in, you are selected.

question: how do i opt into vins?
answer: tell yaksha in the chat: 'i want to take up the online internship without stipend.'

question: when do i get the offer letter?
answer: your offer letter is issued automatically once you upload your signed institutional noc.

question: will i get a certificate?
answer: yes — every intern who completes the internship gets a certificate from vicharanashala, iit ropar.

question: how do i confirm my internship dates?
answer: once you have opted into vins in the chat with yaksha, log in to samagama.in.

question: i am a minor/major in ai student, can i join the programme?
answer: minor/major in ai course from iit ropar is a certification course with a different track.

question: how do i accept the offer letter?
answer: acceptance happens entirely on your dashboard at samagama.in.

question: can i change my internship dates?
answer: before the offer letter is issued: yes. after: no.

question: my noc is not ready but my start date is approaching. what do i do?
answer: get your signed institutional noc uploaded as soon as you can.

question: when does my internship actually begin? will i receive a notification on the day?
answer: your internship begins on the start date you confirmed on the dashboard.

question: can i switch from vins (online) to vise (offline) after being selected?
answer: the two tracks are finalised at the interview stage, and we do not move candidates between them.

question: can i change my internship dates after the offer letter?
answer: no. once your offer letter has been issued, the dates you confirmed are final.

question: what will i work on?
answer: a real open-source project from vicharanashala's portfolio.

question: how many hours per day?
answer: plan for 6 to 10 hours a day, sometimes more during the build phase.

question: who is my mentor?
answer: you will work with the lab's research and engineering team.

question: is there a stipend?
answer: no. the internship is unpaid.

question: do i need my own laptop? should i preload any software?
answer: yes — a personal laptop is required.

question: i am using a different email on github / zoom / the learning platform. is that okay?
answer: no. your registered email is your sole identifier across all programme platforms.

question: why has my mentor not been assigned yet, or contacted me on day 1?
answer: mentors are not assigned on day 1 of the internship.

question: what are the official communication channels?
answer: official channels only. the announcements section on samagama.in is how we notify you.

question: my interview is not marked as complete on the dashboard — what do i do?
answer: a data-sync issue sometimes occurs where the chat session closes but the interview record doesn't update.

question: does vicharanashala send a grade report or evaluation to my university for internship credit?
answer: vicharanashala does not send formal evaluation or grade reports to universities.

question: does the vicharanashala internship certificate specify whether it was completed online or offline?
answer: the certificate you receive on completing the internship is the same for both tracks.

question: will the completion certificate be a physical hardcopy or an e-certificate?
answer: the completion certificate is issued as an e-certificate.

question: is there a whatsapp group for candidates during the internship?
answer: no. see section 6.1 for the official communication channels.

question: what is rosetta?
answer: rosetta is your internship journal — a 65-day document, one entry per day.

question: why does this exist? is it just busywork?
answer: no. it exists for two reasons: for you and for us.

question: what is a 'thinking routine'?
answer: each day in rosetta has a thinking routine — a short framework that gives your reflection a specific shape.

question: how do i get my rosetta journal?
answer: the rosetta template is available as a google doc — make a copy and save it to your drive.

question: how do i use it day to day?
answer: open your rosetta google doc, scroll to the entry for today's day number, fill in the date, read the thinking routine, answer the three prompts.

question: how long should each entry be?
answer: there is no minimum or maximum word count. three to five sentences per prompt is usually enough.

question: what is the one rule?
answer: write what is true. not what sounds impressive.

question: can i use chatgpt or any ai tool to write my entries?
answer: no. this is the one firm rule of rosetta.

question: what if i miss a day?
answer: fill it in as soon as you can. write the actual date you are filling it in.

question: will anyone read my journal during the internship?
answer: no. we will not access your journal during the 65 days.

question: can the prompts change mid-internship?
answer: occasionally we may update a prompt for a specific day.

question: how do i submit rosetta at the end?
answer: on or before day 65, share your rosetta google doc with the programme coordinator's email.

question: i have a question about rosetta that is not answered here. what do i do?
answer: ask yaksha first.

question: my college requires a written confirmation that the internship is self-paced — what document can i share?
answer: this is not a self-paced internship, but a very rigorous and time-demanding one.

question: i've already completed a course with you in an earlier cohort — am i exempt from repeating it?
answer: yes. if you completed the vinternship, pinternship, mern, or ai course with us in an earlier cohort, you don't have to repeat it.

question: how do i register for the ai fundamentals course on vibe?
answer: click the ai fundamentals registration link posted in the announcements section on samagama.in.

question: i registered on vibe with a different email than my samagama email — is that ok?
answer: please use the same email on both platforms.

question: are live sessions mandatory if i'm on the viva route?
answer: yes — live sessions are mandatory for every intern, regardless of path.

question: when and how do i get the zoom link for the kickoff meeting?
answer: the kickoff orientation zoom link is delivered through email and your yaksha chat portal.

question: how do i get the link for the daily zoom standups? are they mandatory?
answer: daily zoom standup links are posted in the announcements section on your samagama.in dashboard.

question: how do i provide my zoom id, and why does it matter?
answer: on your dashboard, just before 'start the internship,' you'll see a step called 'provide your zoom id.'

question: i saved the wrong zoom id — can i change it?
answer: once saved, your zoom id is final and cannot be changed by you.

question: can we register and start the vibe courses before our internship date formally starts?
answer: you will receive the vibe course link only after your internship starts.

question: what are the attendance and participation rules?
answer: attendance and participation are tracked strictly — at least 85% live-session attendance, 85% poll participation, and quizzes passed at 50%.

question: what are spurti points (sp)? do they affect my internship?
answer: spurti points are a platform feature that tracks your engagement with the programme.

question: what are the live-session (zoom) participation and conduct rules?
answer: treat every live session as a professional classroom.

question: i got 'failed to submit poll. error: 100035000' during a session — what does it mean?
answer: this error appears when the poll closed before your response was submitted.

question: what are spurti points?
answer: spurti points, or sp, are a points layer on the platform that reflects your overall engagement.

question: is sp a finished system?
answer: no. spurti points are still being actively built and refined.

question: how much importance should i give to my sp number?
answer: please do not read too much into the number. sp is an early beta feature.

question: can i be terminated or excused because of low sp?
answer: no. the programme team will not terminate or excuse any intern on the basis of spurti points alone.

question: what if my sp shows as zero or even negative?
answer: there is genuinely no cause for concern.

question: does a higher sp bring any benefits?
answer: yes, in a positive way. interns who build up higher spurti points may become eligible for small perks.

question: if sp does not determine outcomes, what does?
answer: your attendance and live participation are what the programme watches closely.

question: what are the participation requirements tracked strictly?
answer: 85% live zoom session time, 85% polls answered, and every quiz attempted and passed at 50%.

question: what does 'rolling basis' mean?
answer: the programme looks at your most recent five working days at any given point in time.

question: what happens if i fall below the required participation level?
answer: you will be moved from the current batch into a later batch.

question: how are spurti points calculated?
answer: every intern starts with a base of 100 sp credited on their official internship start date.

question: i'm unable to type in the chat after clicking 'interact with yaksha' — what should i do?
answer: the chat input is only active after you have clicked the 'interact with yaksha' button.

question: how do i log in to vibe?
answer: link for the website: https://vibe.vicharanashala.ai/auth — sign up as a student with your registered email.

question: invite accepted but shows 'no course enrolled'?
answer: make sure you are logged in with the correct registered email id.

question: why are videos stuck or repeating?
answer: this may happen due to vibe's monitored learning system.

question: can i use a mobile or tablet?
answer: no, only desktop/laptop is supported.

question: i'm experiencing video issues (stuck, looping, skipping) on vibe. how do i troubleshoot?
answer: try these troubleshooting steps in order: refresh, inspect browser console, log out and in, use a different browser, clear cache.

question: i completed all videos and quizzes but my progress is still less than 100%. what should i do?
answer: please do not worry. verify you've completed all course items (1006/1006). try refreshing or clearing cache.

question: i feel the vibe content or platform is not good or i am unhappy. can i request an exception?
answer: vibe is built and continuously improved by interns and students themselves.

question: is the vibe consent form compulsory? what if i don't want to grant camera access?
answer: yes — the consent form is compulsory.

question: what are penalty scores on the vibe platform, and how do they affect our performance?
answer: penalty scores are generated when anomalies are detected during your activity on the vibe platform.

question: when should i use the flag option on vibe, and when should i contact support?
answer: use the flag feature on vibe only for course content-related issues.

question: what is linear progression on vibe?
answer: linear progression is enabled for every course on vibe — each item must be completed in order.

question: can i use the left navigation panel to jump ahead to a later video or quiz?
answer: no. the left navigation panel is meant only as a progress map, not a jump-to-anywhere menu.

question: i am seeing a red 'access restricted' banner. is this a bug?
answer: no, this is not a bug. it appears when you try to open an item before completing all prior items.

question: how do i resolve the 'access restricted' error?
answer: go back to the left panel, find the item without a completion tick, complete it, then refresh.

question: why does vibe sometimes make me re-watch a clip after a quiz?
answer: if your answer to the check-in quiz didn't go through correctly, vibe will take you back to the same clip.

question: what kinds of quiz questions will i see on vibe?
answer: vibe quizzes come in four formats: pick one (mcq), pick one or more (msq), type a number (nat), true or false.

question: are the same proctoring rules applied to every course on vibe?
answer: no — vibe's proctoring system is modular and instructor-controlled.

question: what does the 'quiet helper' on vibe actually do?
answer: a small helper runs on your device using your camera and microphone to check basic learning conditions.

question: does vibe record long videos of me while i'm learning?
answer: no. vibe does not continuously record videos of you.

question: what is the single most common avoidable mistake learners make?
answer: sitting with a window directly behind you during the day, making your face appear as a dark silhouette.

question: why does the lesson keep pausing or restarting even when i'm paying attention?
answer: the cause is almost always something small in your environment, not the platform itself.

question: can i read the quiz questions aloud or mutter to myself while watching?
answer: it's best not to. the microphone listens for sustained voices in the room.

question: can i study with a friend on camera since we're learning together?
answer: in most courses, no. only the registered learner should be in the camera frame.

question: will i lose my progress if i clear my browser or reinstall it?
answer: no. your progress is saved on the server, tied to your registered email.

question: is there a recommended daily learning rhythm on vibe?
answer: yes — small, regular sessions work far better than long marathon sessions.

question: what should my 'study corner' look like before i start a vibe session?
answer: light in front of your face, just you in the camera frame, and a reasonably quiet room.

question: i'm facing a technical issue on vibe that i can't resolve. is there live support available?
answer: yes. a live support breakout session is held every day at 2:00 pm.

question: is team formation compulsory?
answer: yes. all projects in phase 2 and phase 3 must be completed in teams.

question: what is the size of a team?
answer: the team size is fixed at four members.

question: how are teams formed?
answer: for students who joined on may 15 and 16: teams were formed through a structured activity. for later joiners: randomly assigned.

question: i started on may 15/16 but couldn't form a team during the activity. what happens now?
answer: you will be randomly assigned to a team.

question: there was a typo in our email addresses during team formation. can we fix it?
answer: no action is required from your side. the administration will verify and match email ids with names.

question: i formed a team with only two members. will it be considered?
answer: no. teams with fewer than four members will be expanded by adding additional members.

question: what if a team member leaves or becomes ineligible during phase 1?
answer: the administration will attempt to assign a replacement member.

question: can i form a team with someone from my own college?
answer: no. teams must consist of members from different institutions.

question: can i form a team with students from my iit mbs cohort?
answer: no. you are encouraged to collaborate with participants outside your existing cohort.

question: can we change our team name after submission?
answer: yes, team names are tentative and can be changed.

question: what if multiple teams choose the same name?
answer: teams will be distinguished using suffixes (e.g., team x-1, team x-2).

question: what should i do if i face issues within my team?
answer: report any concerns immediately to your assigned scholar/mentor.

question: how will i know who my mentor is?
answer: your mentor will be the scholar assigned to the project your team is working on.

question: when will i know my team details?
answer: team details are announced in the announcements section on samagama.in.

question: i received a team list email but my name is not included. what should i do?
answer: team announcements are phased, so your name may appear in a later list.

question: we selected project x as our top priority but were assigned project y. can we change it?
answer: no. project assignments are final and cannot be changed.

question: i just started the internship. can i form my own team now?
answer: no. for later cohorts, teams will be randomly assigned.

question: when do team activities begin?
answer: team-based work begins in phase 2.

question: can i request a specific teammate after teams are assigned?
answer: no. team assignments are final and requests for changes are not entertained.

question: what happens if a team member is inactive or not contributing?
answer: you should report the issue to your mentor/scholar early.

question: can i switch teams if there are conflicts?
answer: team switches are not allowed except in exceptional, admin-approved cases.

question: will team performance affect individual evaluation?
answer: yes. team deliverables are a key part of evaluation.

question: how will communication happen within teams?
answer: teams self-organise over linkedin or email only. whatsapp groups are prohibited.

question: what if i miss the team allocation announcement?
answer: all programme updates are posted in the announcements section on samagama.in.

question: can a team be dissolved and reformed?
answer: no. once finalized, teams are locked and cannot be dissolved.

question: what happens if i drop out of the internship?
answer: your team will be adjusted accordingly.

question: will we get time to get to know our teammates before phase 2?
answer: yes. there is typically a buffer period before phase 2 where teams can connect and prepare.
